titleStmt.title: The Ambassador in Russia ( (Francis) ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Ambassador in Russia ( Francis ) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 273. Cable filed 3 a.m., 16th, unnumbered because files locked. Moscow-Petrograd papers 15th contained substance of Chicherin Soviet Commissar for Foreign Affairs. note to English and state similar notes forwarded American and French Consuls. Observe British and American notes demand war vessels leave Russian ports while French note mentions Arctic ports only.

Nothing from General Poole
Maj. Gen. Frederick C. Poole, commanding the British forces in north Russia. or Emerson. Col. George Emerson, in charge of the Railway Service Corps.

Reports from various sources confirm arriving of German prisoners to suppress Czechs and in my judgment demand to clear from Russian ports dictated by Germany with which Soviet government apparently reached agreement.

… commissioner mentioned in mine of the [11th] entered a protest through the military attaché; has not replied as he promised; asked me whether preferred proposition from Soviet government or
oppositions thereto and when I replied to bring propositions from both he readily agreed. He asserted that the situation is desperate and I assured him we would relieve famine if possible regardless of dominating authority. Again I urge prompt forwarding of flour to Archangel advising me thereof so I can announce to muzhik people that America has relief en route as such statement would have widespread effect in connection with Allied intervention.
Shall await instructions concerning reply to demand that our war vessels leave Russian ports.
Were we represented in Paris Allied Military Conference June 3; do we accept conclusion thereof?
